goal: Drive P1-W3-F01 (daemon process lifecycle: PID file + SIGTERM/SIGHUP clean shutdown) and P1-W4-F07 (session state tracking: call history, inferred domain, files_in_context, TTL expiry) to passes=true on a FRESH branch. Supersedes the abandoned WO-0008, which landed functionally-correct code (lifecycle::* 7/7 pass, session_manager::test_session_state_tracking 1/1 pass per rejection commit b512bfb) but was rejected 3× on the per-WO cargo-mutants 70% floor — a gate that no longer exists in the verifier cycle per DEC-0007 (accepted 2026-04-17, 50%-floor cargo-mutants now deferred to the Phase 8 release one-shot). Runs in parallel to WO-0020 on a different crate (ucil-daemon vs ucil-core/knowledge_graph.rs); no shared files, no merge hazard.
plan_summary: Part 1 (crates/ucil-daemon/src/lifecycle.rs, NEW module): `PidFile` guard that writes `std::process::id()` to a caller-supplied path (typically `.ucil/daemon.pid`) via synchronous std::fs (PID-file writes happen exactly once at startup, so std::fs is fine — master-plan §11 line 1064 only specifies the pathname), reads the PID back as u32, and removes the file via a best-effort Drop impl. `ShutdownReason { Sigterm, Sighup }` enum. `wait_for_shutdown()` async helper that uses `tokio::signal::unix::signal` for both SIGTERM and SIGHUP and returns the first signal received via `tokio::select!`. `Lifecycle` struct holds a `PidFile` and exposes `run_until_shutdown()` that awaits `wait_for_shutdown()`. `PidFileError` via thiserror with `Io { path, source }` and `Stale { path, pid }` variants. Tests live module-root: pid-file write/read/drop/double-write + shutdown_reason_debug; real signal delivery stays out of unit tests (use tempfile::TempDir, never /tmp). Part 2 (crates/ucil-daemon/src/session_manager.rs, EXTEND): add `CallRecord { tool: String, at: u64 }` + `pub const DEFAULT_TTL_SECS: u64 = 3600;` + extend `SessionInfo` with `call_history: Vec<CallRecord>`, `inferred_domain: Option<String>`, `files_in_context: BTreeSet<PathBuf>` (BTreeSet for deterministic iteration in tests), `expires_at: u64` (unix secs, default `created_at + DEFAULT_TTL_SECS`). Add `SessionManager::{record_call, add_file_to_context, set_inferred_domain, set_ttl, purge_expired}` methods. Add EXACTLY ONE new `#[tokio::test] async fn test_session_state_tracking()` at MODULE LEVEL (NOT inside the existing `#[cfg(test)] mod tests { … }` block — this is the frozen-selector trap that bit WO-0007 and was the original DEC-0005 lesson; `session_manager::test_session_state_tracking` is an exact path match and nextest treats `::tests::` as a distinct segment). Part 3 (crates/ucil-daemon/src/lib.rs): `pub mod lifecycle;` + re-exports. No touch to main.rs, storage.rs, server.rs, plugin_manager.rs. No SQLite work. No watcher work.
scope_in: crates/ucil-daemon/src/lifecycle.rs — NEW file. `PidFileError` (thiserror) with `#[error("pid-file io at {path}: {source}")] Io { path: PathBuf, #[source] source: std::io::Error }` and `#[error("stale pid-file at {path}: pid={pid} no longer live")] Stale { path: PathBuf, pid: u32 }`; mark `#[non_exhaustive]`. `PidFile` struct with `pub fn write(path: &Path) -> Result<Self, PidFileError>` — uses `std::fs::OpenOptions::new().write(true).create(true).truncate(true).open(path)` + writes `std::process::id()` as decimal; `pub fn read(path: &Path) -> Result<u32, PidFileError>`; Drop impl calls `let _ = std::fs::remove_file(&self.path);` (best-effort, never panic in Drop). `#[must_use]` on `PidFile::write`. `ShutdownReason { Sigterm, Sighup }` enum derives `Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq`. `pub async fn wait_for_shutdown() -> std::io::Result<ShutdownReason>` uses `tokio::signal::unix::{signal, SignalKind}` for SIGTERM (`SignalKind::terminate()`) and SIGHUP (`SignalKind::hangup()`), selects the first recv via `tokio::select!`. `Lifecycle` holds a `PidFile` and exposes `pub async fn run_until_shutdown(&self) -> std::io::Result<ShutdownReason>`. Rustdoc on every pub item, citing master-plan §11 line 1064 (daemon.pid) and §18 Phase 1 line 1740 (process lifecycle). Unit tests at MODULE ROOT (not inside `mod tests { }`): `test_pid_file_write_creates_file_with_current_pid`, `test_pid_file_read_returns_written_pid`, `test_pid_file_drop_removes_file`, `test_pid_file_double_write_is_idempotent_or_errors_cleanly`, `test_shutdown_reason_debug_is_readable`. Tests use `tempfile::TempDir::new()`; do NOT hardcode `/tmp`. Do NOT send real SIGTERM in a unit test (real-signal-delivery integration test is out of scope; add later in `crates/ucil-daemon/tests/lifecycle_signals.rs` as a follow-up WO if needed).

crates/ucil-daemon/src/session_manager.rs — EXTEND (do not replace). Add `#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)] pub struct CallRecord { pub tool: String, pub at: u64 }`. Add `pub const DEFAULT_TTL_SECS: u64 = 3600;`. Extend the existing `SessionInfo` struct with four new fields: `#[serde(default)] pub call_history: Vec<CallRecord>`, `#[serde(default)] pub inferred_domain: Option<String>`, `#[serde(default)] pub files_in_context: std::collections::BTreeSet<std::path::PathBuf>`, `#[serde(default)] pub expires_at: u64`. Default-construct in `create_session`: empty vec, None, empty BTreeSet, `created_at + DEFAULT_TTL_SECS`. Add the following `impl SessionManager` async methods (each locks the existing `Arc<RwLock<HashMap<SessionId, SessionInfo>>>` write guard once; return `Option<()>` for missing-id or `usize` for purge): `pub async fn record_call(&self, id: &SessionId, tool: &str) -> Option<()>` (pushes a `CallRecord { tool: tool.to_string(), at: now_unix_secs() }`), `pub async fn add_file_to_context(&self, id: &SessionId, file: PathBuf) -> Option<()>` (BTreeSet insert — de-dupes), `pub async fn set_inferred_domain(&self, id: &SessionId, domain: String) -> Option<()>`, `pub async fn set_ttl(&self, id: &SessionId, ttl_secs: u64) -> Option<()>` (recomputes `expires_at = created_at + ttl_secs`), `pub async fn purge_expired(&self, now_secs: u64) -> usize` (iterates once under write lock, removes entries where `expires_at <= now_secs`, returns count). Add a small free `fn now_unix_secs() -> u64` helper using `std::time::SystemTime::now().duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH).map(|d| d.as_secs()).unwrap_or(0)` (preserves existing error-handling pattern; `?` is not available in a function that returns u64). Add EXACTLY ONE new test `test_session_state_tracking` at MODULE LEVEL with `#[cfg(test)] #[tokio::test] async fn test_session_state_tracking() { … }` — placed BEFORE the existing `#[cfg(test)] mod tests { … }` block, NOT inside it. The frozen selector is the exact path `session_manager::test_session_state_tracking`; nesting produces `session_manager::tests::test_session_state_tracking` and the selector will NOT match (see WO-0007 rejection, escalation 20260415-1856, DEC-0005). The test must: (1) create a session, (2) record two calls via `record_call` and assert `call_history.len() == 2` and tool names match insertion order, (3) add two files via `add_file_to_context` where the second is a duplicate and assert `files_in_context.len() == 2` (BTreeSet de-dupes identical paths — so pass two distinct paths, then re-add one of them, and assert size stays 2), (4) set inferred_domain via `set_inferred_domain` and assert round-trip, (5) call `set_ttl(id, 1)` then `purge_expired(session.created_at + 2)` and assert the return value is 1 AND a subsequent `get_session(&id)` returns None.

crates/ucil-daemon/src/lib.rs — ADD `pub mod lifecycle;` declaration and `pub use lifecycle::{Lifecycle, PidFile, PidFileError, ShutdownReason};` re-exports. Extend the existing `session_manager` re-export line to also export `CallRecord` and `DEFAULT_TTL_SECS`.
scope_out: Do NOT wire main.rs to call Lifecycle::run — leave main.rs byte-for-byte unchanged. Main-wiring integrates lifecycle + watcher + MCP server and is a separate Phase 1 Week 3 WO (P1-W3-F08 territory).

Do NOT implement double-fork(2) daemonization. The `daemonize` requirement in the P1-W3-F01 feature description is satisfied by PID-file + signal-handling alone (master-plan §11 line 1064 specifies only `daemon.pid`). A full double-fork would require an ADR.

Do NOT touch crates/ucil-treesitter/**, crates/ucil-core/**, crates/ucil-lsp-diagnostics/**, crates/ucil-embeddings/**, crates/ucil-agents/**, crates/ucil-cli/**. (ucil-core is actively being edited on feat/WO-0020 — touching it would create a merge hazard.)

Do NOT touch crates/ucil-daemon/src/storage.rs, crates/ucil-daemon/src/server.rs, crates/ucil-daemon/src/plugin_manager.rs, or crates/ucil-daemon/src/main.rs. Stay in lifecycle.rs (new), session_manager.rs (extend), lib.rs (module decl + re-export).

Do NOT modify the existing `#[cfg(test)] mod tests { … }` block contents in session_manager.rs. Those tests satisfy the P1-W2-F05 selector `session_manager::` and touching them risks regressing an already-passing feature. ADD next to it, do not edit it.
Do NOT add `notify` / `notify-debouncer-full` — that is P1-W3-F02 (separate WO, blocked on P1-W2-F06).
Do NOT add `salsa` — that is P1-W3-F04 (already passes).
Do NOT add `plugin.toml` parsing / process spawning — plugin-manager work is in P1-W3-F05/F06 (already passes).

Do NOT touch tests/fixtures/**, ucil-build/feature-list.json, ucil-build/feature-list.schema.json, scripts/gate/**, scripts/flip-feature.sh, ucil-master-plan-v2.1-final.md.
Do NOT send real SIGTERM in a unit test; keep signal-delivery integration tests out of this WO. An integration test that spawns a child process and signals it can be a later WO if gate needs it.

Do NOT attempt to work on the `feat/WO-0008-daemon-lifecycle-session-state` branch — that branch is the abandoned-but-functionally-correct attempt preserved for historical reference. The fresh `feat/WO-0021-daemon-lifecycle-and-session-state` branch starts clean from `main` so no stale state is inherited.

Do NOT use `#[cfg(test)] mod tests { }` to wrap the NEW tests. Flat module-root tests are required per DEC-0005 and the frozen selector `session_manager::test_session_state_tracking`. The EXISTING `mod tests { }` block stays intact and keeps its current contents.

awk -v test_ln="$(grep -n 'fn test_session_state_tracking' crates/ucil-daemon/src/session_manager.rs | head -1 | cut -d: -f1)" -v mod_ln="$(grep -n '^mod tests' crates/ucil-daemon/src/session_manager.rs | head -1 | cut -d: -f1)" 'BEGIN{ if (test_ln == "" || mod_ln == "") exit 1; if (test_ln+0 > mod_ln+0) exit 1; exit 0 }'
test $(git diff origin/main..HEAD -- crates/ucil-core crates/ucil-treesitter crates/ucil-lsp-diagnostics crates/ucil-embeddings crates/ucil-agents crates/ucil-cli | wc -l) -eq 0
test $(git diff origin/main..HEAD -- crates/ucil-daemon/src/main.rs crates/ucil-daemon/src/storage.rs crates/ucil-daemon/src/server.rs crates/ucil-daemon/src/plugin_manager.rs | wc -l) -eq 0
implementation_notes: SIGNAL HANDLING: build the two signal streams once and select on them: `let mut term = signal(SignalKind::terminate()).map_err(...)?; let mut hup = signal(SignalKind::hangup()).map_err(...)?; tokio::select! { _ = term.recv() => ShutdownReason::Sigterm, _ = hup.recv() => ShutdownReason::Sighup }`. No `tokio::time::timeout` needed here — the whole point is to block until a signal arrives. If the daemon wants a shutdown deadline, that is wrapped at the caller, not inside wait_for_shutdown.

FROZEN-SELECTOR TRAP (the one that bit WO-0007 → DEC-0005 → WO-0008 retries): the P1-W4-F07 selector `session_manager::test_session_state_tracking` is an EXACT nextest test-path match; nesting the test inside `#[cfg(test)] mod tests { }` produces `session_manager::tests::test_session_state_tracking` and nextest treats `::tests::` as a distinct path segment — the selector will NOT match, even though the test itself passes. The executor MUST place the new test at MODULE ROOT, next to the existing `mod tests { }` block, not inside it. Verify with `grep -n 'fn test_session_state_tracking'` and `grep -n '^mod tests'` — the fn line number must be LESS than the mod line number.

PID FILE SEMANTICS: `PidFile::write` uses synchronous `std::fs::OpenOptions::new().write(true).create(true).truncate(true).open(path)`. This is acceptable because PID-file writes happen exactly once at daemon startup; using tokio::fs here would be pointless overhead. The `Drop` impl runs on the caller thread (which may be async) but only invokes `std::fs::remove_file` synchronously — that is a cheap system call and does not need to be awaited.

SESSIONINFO BACKWARDS COMPAT: annotate all four new fields with `#[serde(default)]` so older SessionInfo JSON blobs (if any exist on disk) continue to deserialise. Document this explicitly in the rustdoc for SessionInfo.

PURGE SEMANTICS: `purge_expired(now_secs)` takes a SINGLE write lock, iterates once, removes entries where `expires_at <= now_secs`, returns the count. Use `HashMap::retain(|_, v| v.expires_at > now_secs)` + a pre-count of size delta OR a Vec of keys-to-remove followed by `.remove()` calls; either works, retain is cleaner.

TTL RECOMPUTATION: `set_ttl(id, ttl_secs)` sets `info.expires_at = info.created_at + ttl_secs`. That means setting a TTL of 1 on a session created at T0 yields expires_at = T0+1, so `purge_expired(T0+2)` removes it. The test MUST mirror this arithmetic: record created_at, call set_ttl(id, 1), call purge_expired(session.created_at + 2), assert returned count == 1.

COMMIT CADENCE (target ~8 commits, each ≤150 lines per the DEC-0001/DEC-0005 module-introduction exception): (1) feat(daemon): add lifecycle module skeleton with PidFile guard, (2) test(daemon): pid-file write/read/drop module-root tests, (3) feat(daemon): add ShutdownReason + wait_for_shutdown via SIGTERM/SIGHUP, (4) test(daemon): lifecycle smoke tests, (5) feat(daemon): add CallRecord + DEFAULT_TTL_SECS + SessionInfo state fields, (6) feat(daemon): add SessionManager state methods (record_call/add_file/set_domain/set_ttl/purge_expired), (7) test(daemon): add module-level test_session_state_tracking, (8) chore(daemon): lib.rs re-exports + clippy cleanup. The `lifecycle.rs` introduction may legitimately exceed 150 lines if type+impl+tests must ship coherently — cite DEC-0005 in the commit body if so. Cap any single commit at 400L per the DEC-0005 soft ceiling.

CRITIC PRE-CHECK: run `cargo clippy -p ucil-daemon --all-targets -- -D warnings` before writing the ready-for-review marker. Common traps: `clippy::missing_errors_doc` on public `Result`-returning fns (add `# Errors` sections in rustdoc), `#[must_use]` on `PidFile::write`, unused imports in lifecycle.rs if you prototype with more types than you use.
NO UNWRAP IN NON-TEST CODE per .claude/rules/rust-style.md. Use `?` with typed errors. In tests, `.expect("msg")` is permitted.

DEC-0007 IS THE UNBLOCKING REASON: the executor may notice that the prior WO-0008 attempt on branch `feat/WO-0008-daemon-lifecycle-session-state` is essentially the same implementation and was rejected on cargo-mutants (41% < 70% floor). Do NOT cherry-pick from that branch. Start fresh from `main` on `feat/WO-0021-daemon-lifecycle-and-session-state` — executor may READ the WO-0008 branch as a reference but must re-implement cleanly. The per-WO cargo-mutants gate is removed per DEC-0007; the remaining gates are (a) functional nextest, (b) clippy, (c) reality-check.sh stash-oracle, (d) coverage-gate (85% line / 75% branch nightly), (e) critic review for real-code-doing-real-work.
